revision_note: v2: Announcements semantic reframed after 2026-05-26 discovery that GitHub no longer supports category-level 'Allow comments' toggle (deprecated mid-2024 — all category formats now allow comments). Replacement guarantee: `format=Announcement` (only maintainers can post new discussions) PLUS every discussion in Announcements MUST be locked-after-publish (blocks non-maintainer commenting per-post). Fixture + discussions_cats.sh + IC-9 + phase-1 Task 1.2 acceptance #3 all updated in lockstep.
purpose: Canonical specification of the 5 Discussions categories per IC-9. tests/repo/discussions_cats.sh asserts the live repo matches. Categories must be created via the GitHub web UI (no public createDiscussionCategory mutation as of 2026-05); use docs/discussions-setup-walkthrough.md as the paste-driven runbook.

categories.name: Announcements
categories.description: One-way broadcasts from the maintainer. Format=Announcement restricts who can post new discussions (maintainers + admins only); each Announcement post is locked after publish to block non-maintainer commenting.
categories.comments_enabled_note: true at the CATEGORY level — GitHub deprecated the per-category 'Allow comments' toggle mid-2024; all formats now allow comments at the category level. Load-bearing replacement is `discussions_lock_after_publish` below.
categories.ui_notes: Create with TYPE = 'Announcement' (do this at category creation; format can be changed later via Edit category but requires no existing discussions in a non-Announcement type first). After each Announcement post is published, lock it: `gh api graphql -f query='mutation { lockLockable(input: {lockableId: "D_kwDO..."}) { lockedRecord { ... on Discussion { locked } } } }'`. discussions_cats.sh asserts all Announcement discussions are locked.

categories.name: Q&A
categories.description: Usage questions and how-to's. Mark the accepted answer with the green check; mark-answer surfaces the best reply for future readers.
categories.ui_notes: Create with TYPE = 'Question / Answer'. The mark-answer feature requires this type.

categories.name: Ideas
categories.description: Feature seedlings, design exploration, things that aren't quite ready to become an Issue. Upvote with thumbs-up.

categories.name: Show & Tell
categories.description: Projects you built with esphome.cloud. Photos, videos, repo links — show off what you made.

categories.name: Solutions Share
categories.description: User-contributed Solution templates: complete board × peripheral × use-case configurations others can re-use directly.

welcome_post.title: Welcome to esphome.cloud / community
welcome_post.body_template:
Welcome! This repository is the single feedback intake for esphome.cloud BETA.

Not sure where to file? The [README decision graph](https://github.com/esphome-cloud/community#what-do-you-want-to-do) maps your intent to one of three channels: **Discussions**, **Issues**, or **Email**. Each has one purpose; each purpose has one channel.

Response times live in [Response Times](https://github.com/esphome-cloud/community#response-times) (TL;DR: security@ within 24 hours every day; everything else on the Tuesday office-hours window, 14:00-16:00 UTC+8).

The AI triage assistant reads every Issue and Discussion within ~90 seconds and either replies with a known-issue fix, asks for missing info, or flags it for human review. Replying re-opens the thread for human review.

Welcome again, and thanks for trying the BETA.
